videos.title: How Have Markets Reacted To Election Verdicts in the Past | India Markets To Rally Further?
videos.text: [Music] stock markets by Nature heavily influenced by both domestic and international events there's no surprise then that the market participants are now eagerly awaiting the June 4th outcome of the general elections here in India which will give markets their purpose and Direction exit polls have already shown a resounding majority for the BJP Le NBA government going by what we saw on 3rd May where Indian markets touch a new all-time high by just reacting to those exit poles word on the street is that there will be a further rally which would be on expect line let me take you down memory lane then to see how much markets have reacted on result day in the past five elections but before that don't forget to like share and subscribe to money control for more such content now in the past markets have more often than not have been quite muted response on result day on the day of counting especially let's go back to 1999 where results were announced on 6th of October with the BJP Le NDA coming to par the sensex on that day was close to 4,948 levels whereas the Nifty was just about 1,465 both the sensex and Nifty saw a 52% of gain on that day the BJP won with just about 182 seats and in the NDA took that count to 270 moving on to 2004 when the results were declared on May 13th but we saw the markets reacting on May 14th with the Nifty and sensex slumping in trade Falling by about 7.87% and 6.10% respective acely Indian National Congress was the one that came to power with a UPA winning about 218 seats the crash could be attributed to the unexpected victory of the Congress L UPA defying expectations of most analysts the market also went further down over the next few trading days as it became clearer that the newly formed coalition government would be supported by the left parties as well now in 2009 results were declared on May 16th which was a market holiday and hence we saw the Indian V Mark indices reacting on the results outcome on May 18th both Traders and investors had enough time then to digest the news the UPA retained power and showed a strength of 262 out of the 543 seats while the Nifty and sensex both surge to such an extent hitting an upper circuit breaker and that trading had to be stopped mid session because of that nonetheless we saw the Nifty gaining about 17.74% to close in at 4,323 .15 levels on that day whereas the sensex sold as much as 14,284 let's move on to 2014 where we saw the BJP L NDA come back to Power on May 16th the NDA got a total of 336 seats within Narendra Modi government taking charge as prime minister markets were quite volatile that day with the benchmarks barely witnessing any much swings the Nifty gained about 1.1% closing in at 7,23 Lev levels whereas it was a sensex which was rather flat at just about 0.90% gaining to about 24,1 121 levels finally let's take a look at 2019 where the markets took a dip on results day many say that this could be because the BJP LED NDA government's victory was already factored in by the market and there was weakness in the larger economic fundamentals which led to investors booking profits leading to that overall fall in the market prices the BJP won with a thumping three 103 seats but the Nifty slipped by 0.69% while the sensex closed at 38811 or 0.76% lower the only other similar instance where the markets dipped the year after the 2019 elections was in 2020 due to the covid-19 pandemic which resulted in the crash of markets across the world now from those 11,600 levels that we were on in 2019 to the 23,200 odd levels that we are hovering around today the markets have seen some interesting swings and whole lot of volatility all eyes are now fixed on June 4th the election outcome that will give markets their much needed momentum

videos.title: Nifty Hits Lifetime High: Sensex Nears All-Time Peak; Breaches September 2024 Level Of 26,277
videos.text: In the markets, bank nifty at all-time high. Uh the nifty starting about 26,200 points. Sensex climbing 125 points. So the key benchmark indices of the Indian stock markets. Sensex and Nifty50 opening higher uh following up global market cues. My colleague Hina Gamir is joining us on the broadcast with the details. Hina, what are the markets cheering? Well, there are a lot of things that markets are cheering this morning. But let's tell you what levels are Sensex and Nifty at right now. As we speak, Nifty in fact has breached its all-time high level of 26,277. It's seen a gain of about 75 odd points. And as far as Sensex is concerned, Pratiba, it is just at a kissing distance away from its all-time high level of 85,978. uh this last all-time high in fact was seen in September last year and from then uh you know we have seen markets not really doing very well because of a number of reason the most important of course one was the tariffs that were imposed on India by uh US President Donald Trump and now we are seeing markets back at all-time high levels now what is the reason behind this exuberance there are a lot of factors fueling this kind of a bullish run in both Sensex as well as Nifty on L Street particularly one of of them is that uh the crude oil prices have been seeing a drop. They are hovering around just six uh $60 per barrel which is good news for a country like India and its macros. So that is one. Apart from that global markets have been doing pretty well. That's lending some positivity here on Bal Street. Then uh tomorrow in fact India's GDP data is expected to be released. Remember first quarter GDP surprised that surprised us at 7.8%. GST bonanza is likely to give us a good number tomorrow as well for the second quarter. So that's another reason why markets are happy. Then progress is being made on India US trade deal. That's another reason why overall investors are feeling good about the sentiment going forward. U you know that is another reason why markets are looking pretty good. And lastly in December some people feel there is a higher possibility of a rate cut by US Fed. So that's another good uh thing that the markets you know are feeling good about and that's the reason why we are seeing this cheer in the market on the last streak last largely Indian markets are following the global trend right now global markets are looking good foreign investors are also putting in a lot of money on Indian stocks in Indian stocks and that's why our markets are looking also pretty good right now >> okay so rate cut rally extending global trends and of course the trade talks between US and India. But Tina, is this sentiment likely to continue? >> Well, uh fingers crossed if the trade deal announcement comes uh soon, then this sentiment is expected to continue. This bullish momentum is expected to continue. Apart from that Pratipha, there is one more reason that markets are pretty happy and that is uh you know perhaps uh uh the Russia Ukraine peace plan that's also being discussed as reports suggest that US has been preparing a peace plan to end the ongoing war. That's another factor that markets are very very uh you know hopeful of and remember markets uh behave as per trends. If there is an anticipation of something happening in a particular event, you will see markets reacting in a certain way. So right now the expectation is that perhaps there's going to be an agreement on peace plan between Russia and Ukraine. So markets feel that that could bring some kind of stability. Nobody really likes wars. Apart from that, remember we've been talking about a trade deal announcement between India and United States of America very very soon. all the positive commentary we've been getting from both the sides on this particular aspect. So the anticipation is that okay soon perhaps in the next few weeks or so we could hear some good news on trade deal front as well between both the countries. So that's something what markets are really looking forward to. Indications are it's going to come sooner than later. That's why you're seeing this positive sentiment on the last three. >> All right, Tina, thank you for joining us with those details. So, some good news coming in from the markets as they're in full momentum. >> [music]

videos.title: Will the Indian stock rally continue? | World Business Watch
videos.text: Indian stocks regained ground to open in the green boy by positive Global cues now this comes a day after they took a breather from record highs in the previous session Benchmark indices Rose in early trade on Thursday the question is will they hit new highs now in the previous session after opening at New Life highs and breaching intraday levels Benchmark indices reversed course to tank sharly as investors booked profits at record high levels the Blue Chip sensex index sank over 500 points after hitting a high of 80481 the index closed marginally below the 80,000 level Wednesday saw the largest drop in the broader nifty50 index since June 4th when an election setback for prime minister Narendra modi's party spooked the market the 1.2% drop in the index came after it hit a new all-time high of 24461 in early trade the index ended nearly 0.5% lower at 24,32 4 the Breather comes after the sensex and the Nifty hit repeated record highs since a nearly $400 billion loss on the election results stay the recovery of the over 5% single day loss in under three sessions marked the fastest rebound rate in over a decade since then the sensex has risen over 8,000 points the Nifty has set some kind of a record in 21 of the 22 sessions since the current rally of new record Milestones began both bench Ben marks have already breached yearend targets pushing analysts to reassess their targets for 2024 and 2025 [Music]

videos.title: How Stock Markets Reacted To Budget In Last 10 Years & What Is This Year's Expectation | Budget 2026
videos.text: On February 1st, everyone's eyes will be glued on their screens with ray of hopes from the finance minister as she will present the union budget for FY27. It's a Sunday, but stock markets will be open for trade on this special day. Investors, traders, market analysts, and corporate leaders will especially keep an eye on the stock market ahead of the budget presentation. The finance minister has already held many budget consultations with various organizations and industries. Those industries and organizations have come out with their expectations and suggestions. How these suggestions will be taken is in the hands of the finance ministry. But in this video we will discuss how the markets moved on budget day in the past 10 years. Union budget of 2016 was presented on February 29 focused on rural development with a plan to double farmers incomes in 5 years. Markets however were unimpressed. Nifty fell 0.6% that day and well well it went up by 10.7% in the following month. Union budget of 2017 presented on 1st February focused on relief to middle class tax was cut to 5% for income between 2.5 lakh rupees to 5 lakh rupees. Markets reacted positively. In fact, Nifty saw a 1.8% jump on the budget day and it went up by 2.1% in the following month too. Union budget of 2018 was presented by Arun Jet Lee and it was the last budget he presented as the finance minister on February 1st 2018. The budget had major proposals for MSME's employment generation and infrastructure. LTCG was introduced in this budget and markets reacted cautiously. Sentiment turned weak as Nifty fell 0.2% on the budget day and 6% in the following month. Union budget of 2019 was presented by Nirmala Sithar Raman on 5th of July in 2019. Push Goyel had presented the interim budget for that year on February 1st increasing defense budget announcing benefits like income tax SOPs for nearly 30 million lowincome taxpayers and 120 million marginal farmers. Sith Raman kept major announcements of the interim budget unchanged but markets were not very impressed and nifty fell 1.1% on the budget day and 8% in the following year. In the union budget of 2020 new tax slabs were introduced while no major boost was given to industries. Markets were in red on budget day. Nifty fell 2.5%. Following month it fell further by 3.1%. In the union budget 2021 market saw a boost on the budget day. Nifty went up by 4.7% and in the following month too it was up by 3.4%. The budget was a revival for pandemic hit economy and highest ever capital outlay was given to infrastructure. In union budget 2022 foundation was laid for economic growth post pandemic. Nifty went up by 1.4% 4% on the budget day but fell 4.5% in the following month. In market had mixed reactions while Sensex closed up slightly 2% nifty fell 0.26% even as measures to boost consumption and capeex were proposed in the following month too. Nifty fell around 1.45%. In 2024's union budget Sensex and Nifty settled marginally lower in volatile trade. Sensex fell 0.0 07% and nifty closed 0.12% on the budget day. In the following month, the Nifty was up 1.41%. In the last year's budget of 2025, Nilasaraman announced massive income tax cut to boost private consumption and that was prioritized over Cape. Benchmark indices ended flat. Sensex ended at 77,5.96 points while Nifty ended at 23,482.15 points. And in the following month, Nifty was in deep red down 5.9%. So in most cases, Nifty and Sensex both saw minor fall. Now this is the data for the markets performance in the previous budgets. How will the markets perform on budget day this year? We don't know. But many experts suggest that investors should stick to stocks that are likely to benefit from government spending. Which stocks and sectors are to watch for? We will tell you in the next week closer to the budget. Till then keep watching ET now for more such news and views and for latest updates on budget 2025. And lastly for the budget trivia question today, what is the full form of LTCG? Let us know in the comments and the right answer gets pinned. >> If you like this video then like, share and subscribe to ET now.
